seo.title: Pydantic AI Guardrails for E-Commerce
seo.description: Compliance guide for Pydantic AI agents in e-commerce. Meet FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: E-Commerce teams using Pydantic AI face heightened compliance risks due to AI-generated product descriptions, pricing calculations, and customer interactions that must adhere to strict FTC, CCPA/GDPR, PCI DSS, and ADA regulations. Without robust guardrails, subtle AI errors like unsubstantiated claims or inaccurate pricing can trigger FTC enforcement, refund disputes, and brand damage. Vex provides production-grade validation as a result_validator, ensuring Pydantic AI outputs comply with e-commerce standards before reaching customers.

complianceRequirements.description: Product claims must be substantiated with evidence; AI-generated descriptions cannot make unverified superiority claims.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex result_validators scan outputs for prohibited phrases like 'best ever' and cross-reference against product data before approval.

complianceRequirements.description: Customer data handling in personalization must include opt-out notices and data minimization.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces privacy policy inclusion in all personalized responses and blocks PII leakage in AI outputs.

complianceRequirements.description: Payment flows must not expose card details; AI cannot generate or log sensitive payment information.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex payment validators reject any response containing card patterns or unencrypted payment references.

complianceRequirements.description: AI-generated content must be WCAG-compliant with proper alt text and semantic structure.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex accessibility checks validate structured outputs for screen reader compatibility and ARIA labels.

industryRisks.name: Unsubstantiated Product Claims
industryRisks.description: AI fabricates benefits not backed by product specs, violating FTC rules.
industryRisks.example: AI claims 'clinically proven to reduce wrinkles' for generic moisturizer.

industryRisks.name: Dynamic Pricing Errors
industryRisks.description: AI miscalculates discounts or taxes, leading to refund disputes.
industryRisks.example: Promised 50% off shows as 45% at checkout due to token truncation.

industryRisks.name: Inaccurate Refund Policy Representation
industryRisks.description: AI summarizes policies incorrectly, causing customer service escalations.
industryRisks.example: AI states 'no questions asked 90-day returns' when policy is 30 days.

industryRisks.name: Privacy Notice Omission
industryRisks.description: Personalized recommendations lack CCPA opt-out language.
industryRisks.example: AI suggests products 'based on your browsing' without data rights notice.

implementation.title: FTC Claims Validator for Product Descriptions
implementation.description: Add Vex result_validator to block unsubstantiated claims in AI-generated descriptions.

implementation.title: Pricing Accuracy Validator
implementation.description: Validate AI-calculated prices against canonical pricing data.
implementation.code:
from vex import Guardrail, Policy
from pydantic_ai import ResultValidator

pricing_guardrail = Guardrail(
    name="ecommerce-pricing-accuracy",
    policy=Policy.BLOCK,
    validators=[
        "calculated_price == canonical_price * (1 + tax_rate)",
        "discount_percentage <= max_allowed_discount"
    ],
    context_providers=["fetch_canonical_price(product_id)"]
)

class PricingAgent(Agent):
    result_validator = ResultValidator(guards=[pricing_guardrail])

    async def calculate_price(self, product_id: str, quantity: int) -> dict:
        return await self._ai_calculate(product_id, quantity)

implementation.title: Privacy Compliance Wrapper
implementation.description: Ensure all personalized responses include CCPA/GDPR notices.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Pydantic AI in E-Commerce: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
FashionRetailCo deployed Pydantic AI agents to generate 10,000+ daily product descriptions, dynamic pricing quotes, and personalized recommendations across their platform. The AI improved conversion rates 18% initially, but subtle compliance failures emerged: product descriptions contained phrases like 'miracle fabric' without substantiation, pricing calculations occasionally mismatched checkout totals due to token limits, and personalized emails omitted CCPA opt-out language. Within 3 months, they faced 247 refund disputes, negative Trustpilot reviews citing 'bait-and-switch pricing,' and a CCPA complaint investigation.

Engineering teams struggled with Pydantic AI's result_validator limitations—the validators passed technically valid JSON but missed business logic errors like FTC violations. Tool retry logic masked persistent pricing calculation failures, and nested recommendation calls lost pricing context, compounding errors. Without production-grade guardrails, the AI's speed advantage became a compliance liability.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex result_validators on all Pydantic AI agents, FashionRetailCo achieved 100% FTC compliance on product claims, eliminated pricing discrepancies, and standardized privacy notices. Vex blocked 4,732 non-compliant responses in the first month while maintaining 98.7% throughput. Refund disputes dropped 89%, Trustpilot scores recovered to 4.6/5, and the CCPA investigation closed without penalties. The Vex + Pydantic AI stack now powers $2.3M monthly revenue with full audit trails for every compliance decision.

cta.heading: Comply with E-Commerce Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Install Vex today with `pip install vex-sdk` and add production-grade guardrails to your Pydantic AI agents in 15 minutes.
